mnemonics.title: NPS Classification
mnemonics.content: P-D-P: Promoters (9-10) are Positive, Passives (7-8) are Passive, Detractors (0-6) are Dismal - think '9-10 are Perfect, 7-8 are Passable, 0-6 are Terrible'

mnemonics.title: Usability Metrics
mnemonics.content: E³: Effectiveness, Efficiency, Enjoyment - 'The 3 Es of Usability' - helps remember core usability metrics

mnemonics.title: SUS Score Interpretation
mnemonics.content: 68 is Average - think 'SUS 68 is Heaven (above average), below is Low' - scores above 68 are above average

mnemonics.title: Likert Scale Order
mnemonics.content: SD-D-N-A-SA: Strongly Disagree → Disagree → Neutral → Agree → Strongly Agree - think 'Disagree, Don't Argue, Stay Assertive'

mnemonics.title: Nielsen's Heuristics
mnemonics.content: VISIBLE: Visibility, Consistency, Error prevention, Feedback, Control, Help, Documentation - think 'Visibility Is System's Key Feature'

acronyms.usage: A 10-question standardized questionnaire to measure perceived usability, score 0-100, above 68 is good
Measures customer loyalty by likelihood to recommend, range -100 to +100
Psychometric scale from 'Strongly Disagree' to 'Strongly Agree', typically 5 or 7 points
A single-question measure of task difficulty, rated from 'Very Easy' to 'Very Difficult'
Quantifiable measure used to evaluate success in meeting objectives
Metric measuring the profitability of UX improvements
Direct measurement of customer satisfaction, typically on 1-5 or 1-10 scale
Post-task question measuring perceived difficulty of just-completed task

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a thermometer from 0-100, with 68 marked as the 'freezing point' - above it is 'warm' (good usability), below is 'cold' (poor usability)
Think of a speedometer from -100 to +100, with 0 as 'neutral'. Green zone (positive) is Promoters, yellow is Passives, red is Detractors
Picture a horizontal line with 5 points: SD (left) → SA (right). The further right, the more positive the attitude being measured.
Use a brain (Attitudinal = what you THINK) vs eye (Behavioral = what you DO) icon distinction. Attitudinal = introspection, Behavioral = observation.
Think of a pie chart - the shaded portion represents successful completions. Higher shaded area = better success rate.
Picture errors as red dots on a path. Error rate = red dots / total dots × 100. Fewer red dots = lower error rate.

analogies.analogy: Like a doctor's diagnostic tools - just as a stethoscope and blood pressure cuff help doctors understand patient health, measurement instruments help UX designers understand user experience health.
SUS is like a student's exam score - 100 is perfect, 68 is the passing/average mark, and anything below needs 'remediation' (design improvements).
Behavioral measures are like security camera footage - objective recordings of actual actions, not what people claim they did.
Attitudinal measures are like a job interview - self-reported opinions and feelings, which may not always match actual behavior.
A/B testing is like a science experiment with control (A) and treatment (B) groups to determine which variable produces better results.
Think-Aloud is like having a tour guide narrate their thinking while showing visitors around - you learn not just where they go but why.
Heuristic Evaluation is like a car safety inspection - experts check against a checklist of known safety standards to identify potential problems before users encounter them.
